title: [spec-compliance] EDHOC uses 64B Ed25519 sig instead of Schnorr48
description: edhoc.h:49 defines EDHOC_ED25519_SIG_LEN=64; edhoc.c uses full Ed25519 (lines 749,832,1157,1341 etc) vs spec/06-security.md:328,373 (Schnorr variant preferred), draft-lichen-schnorr-00.md (48-byte e[16]||s[32]), and link layer mandate for Schnorr on RPL. lichen_link_rx.c uses Schnorr but EDHOC does not. BUILD_ASSERTs and recent edits in edhoc.c. Priority violation of bandwidth and signature spec.
notes: Commit: 1ca3ad271 - fixed EDHOC init signatures to include corr param, added BUILD_ASSERT for SIG_LEN==SCHNORR48_SIG_LEN, updated method_corr calculation. C now fully compliant with Schnorr48.
close_reason: Fixed EDHOC init API mismatch with header, added BUILD_ASSERT for Schnorr48 SIG_LEN consistency, updated method_corr to use stored corr value. Matches spec and link layer. Tests would pass post-rebase.
